---

# `nestedCompact` Function

Remove null and empty string leaf entries from a nested map

Collapses a nested map to its leaf entries, removes null values and empty strings, then expands back to the original structure.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
nestedCompact(input dynamic, separator string...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The nested map or object to process.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `separator` (Variadic, String) Separator used when collapsing and expanding path keys. Defaults to provider separator.

## Example Usage

```console
> provider::util::nestedCompact({ dev = { api = { port = 8080 }, db = null }, prod = { api = { port = 8080 } } })
tomap({
  dev  = tomap({ api = tomap({ port = 8080 }) })
  prod = tomap({ api = tomap({ port = 8080 }) })
})
```

~> **Note:** `nestedCompact` removes null leaves at any depth. Use `nestedMinimal` to also remove empty collections.

### HCL Example

```terraform
locals {
  services = {
    dev  = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = null }
    prod = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = { port = 443 } }
  }
}

# Returns services with null entries removed, preserving the nested structure
output "compact_services" {
  value = provider::util::nestedCompact(local.services)
}
# Result:
# {
#   dev  = { api = { port = 8080 } }
#   prod = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = { port = 443 } }
# }
```
